title: Mehli Mistry resigns from Ratan Tata's family office
content:
• Tata Trusts, comprising Sir Dorabji Tata Trust (27.98%) and Sir Ratan Tata Trust (23.56%), collectively own a majority 65.9% stake in Tata Sons.
• RNT Associates Pvt Ltd, established in March 2009 as Ratan Tata's investment vehicle for startups, generated ₹36 crore in revenue for the year ended March 2023.
• Mehli Mistry resigned from RNT Associates Pvt Ltd, effective July 1, 2026, after his ouster from the Tata Trusts in November 2025.

title: Fed Kevin Warsh says inflation risks have come down, vows, 'We're going to deliver price stability'
content:
• Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh reiterated the US central bank's determination to bring inflation back to its 2% target.
• Fed policymakers, under Kevin Warsh, have agreed that 'forward guidance' is not well-suited to the current policy conjuncture, signaling a shift in communication strategy.
• The next Federal Reserve policy decision refers to the July 28-29 meeting, where half of 18 officials projected a rate increase this year.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 celebrations turn fatal in Mexico as three die of suffocation after El Tri advance into Round of 16
content:
• Mexico achieved its first FIFA World Cup knockout win in over 40 years, defeating Ecuador 2-0 in the Round of 32 of the FIFA World Cup 2026.
• Three persons died of suffocation during mass celebrations in Mexico City after Mexico's victory in the FIFA World Cup 2026.
• Ecuador's coach, Sebastián Beccacece, resigned after their FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 loss, having led the team for two years.

title: Parliament Standing Committee on Finance to review Virtual Digital Assets; RBI, ICAI to take part
content:
• The Standing Committee on Finance will hold a sitting on July 2, 2026, to study the regulatory roadmap for Virtual Digital Assets (VDAs), with representatives from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) participating.
• India currently taxes Virtual Digital Assets (VDAs) at 30% on gains and imposes a 1% TDS on transfers, rules that were introduced in 2022.
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) will present its assessment of VDAs, covering financial stability risks, monetary policy impact, money laundering concerns, and investor protection, having previously cautioned against private cryptocurrencies.

title: India unlikely to raise inflation target, RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra says; expects strong growth
content:
• In March, the Indian government retained the retail inflation target at 4%, within its comfort band of 2%-6%.
• The retail inflation target of 4% within the 2%-6% band will remain in place for five years.
• India's retail inflation stood at 3.93% in May, and the economy grew 7.8% year-on-year in the quarter ended March.

title: Growth engine revving as GST, auto sales rise despite global roadblocks
content:
• Gross goods and services tax (GST) collections rose 13.9% to Rs 1.95 lakh crore in June.
• The central bank has cut its FY27 growth forecast to 6.6% from the earlier forecast of 6.9%.
• Gross GST collections in the first quarter (Q1) reached Rs 6.32 lakh crore, up 8.4% from a year earlier.

title: New rural job scheme VB-G RAM-G pays 10% more than MGNREGA
content:
• The G RAM-G daily wage has been raised to ₹327.4 from ₹298.8 (under MGNREGA), and a new base wage of ₹300 a day has been introduced, benefiting 21 states and Union territories.
• The annual employment guarantee under the new scheme has been raised to 125 days per rural household from 100 days under MGNREGA.
• States (except those in the Northeast and hilly regions) will now bear 40% of the wage cost, with the Centre funding the remainder.

title: JSW Infra raises ₹7,503 crore via QIP to fund capex plan
content:
• JSW Infrastructure successfully raised ₹7,503 crore through a Qualified Institutional Placement (QIP).
• This QIP by JSW Infrastructure marks the first transaction in India to combine a primary issuance and an offer-for-sale by a promoter selling shareholder within a single QIP structure.
• The QIP included a primary issuance of securities worth ₹6,555 crore, with the funds intended to support JSW Infrastructure's ₹39,000 crore multi-year capital expenditure programme.

title: Validity of FY26 gold import quota licences under India-UAE trade pact extended till September 30
content:
• The validity of gold import quota licences issued under the India-UAE Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) for the financial year 2025-26 has been automatically extended until September 30, 2026.
• The India-UAE CEPA was implemented in May 2022, allowing India to import up to 200 metric tonnes of gold annually from the UAE with a one per cent tariff concession.
• India's total gold imports last fiscal (2025-26) amounted to approximately USD 72 billion, representing a 24 per cent year-on-year increase from USD 58 billion in 2024-25.

title: Why July rainfall matters for India's economy and farmers
content:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast below-normal rainfall for July.
• July is identified as the most crucial month for India's southwest monsoon and kharif sowing.
• Below-normal rainfall in July could have implications for agriculture, food inflation, farm incomes, and economic growth in India.
